publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2020-04-09 23:54:50 -0400.

change.item: This resource was modified by the ArchivesSpace Preprocessor developed by the Harvard Library (https://github.com/harvard-library/archivesspace-preprocessor)

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

origination.label: creator
origination.corpname: Cornell University. Department of Housing and Design

did.abstract: Black and white glass lantern slides depicting prefabricated housing to room interiors and furniture were used by Loper for teaching and research. Some were made commercially and some taken by Cornell photographer Jon Troy.

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu

bioghist.head: BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE
bioghist.p: Loper was New York state's first female extension architect and worked at Cornell from 1946-1967.

scopecontent.head: COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
scopecontent.p: Black and white glass lantern slides depicting prefabricated housing to room interiors and furniture were used by Loper for teaching and research. Some were made commercially and some taken by Cornell photographer Jon Troy.

emph.ref: Series III. Exhibit Design: The Museum of Modern Art
Series VI. House Planning, Insulation, Heating, Kitchens

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: Ruby Loper slide collection, #23-16-3265. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

did.unittitle: Troy Studio, Lantern Slide Makers, Cornell University, Ithaca, N.Y.

did.unittitle: (Pre-fabricated houses are now in the air)

did.unittitle: Coordination of Men + Materials + Time = Prefabrication

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Diagrammatical explanation of how men, materials, and time are the resources for producing prefabricated homes

did.unittitle: Commercial Standard CS125-47 for Prefabricated Homes

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Gives a statement of 'purpose' and 'scope'; statements of 'definition' and 'general requirements' are partially cut out

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Aerial view of a large factory, the Gunnison Plant, in New Albany, Indiana

scopecontent.p: Interior view of men working on factory lines

scopecontent.p: Interior view of factory; wall panels are finished and dried

scopecontent.p: Interior view of factory; women work on wall panels attached to overhead conveyors; windows are installed in the panels

scopecontent.p: Interior view of factory; men and women prepare and pack construction materials into two large boxes

scopecontent.p: Exterior view of tractor trailers labeled 'Gunnison Homes' lined up outside the factory for loading and shipment

did.unittitle: Series VI. House Planning, Insulation, Heating and Kitchens
Glass slides pertaining to house planning, insulation, heating, and kitchens
